palette: Moss green, charcoal, raw concrete, and a thin sliver of neon magenta.
lighting: Late afternoon dappled sunlight filtering through dense green canopy; high contrast between deep shadows and bright organic textures.
instagram_prompts: First-person perspective (FPP) optic sensor capture. A high-detail close-up of a raw concrete balcony edge at TRUNK(HOTEL) YOYOGI PARK. In the background, the dense, vibrant green canopy of Yoyogi Park is slightly blurred, with dappled sunlight creating intricate patterns of light and shadow. A small, sleek matte charcoal sensor is barely visible on the concrete surface. The atmosphere is quiet, sophisticated, and intentional. World is impossibly large. No text or overlays.

First-person perspective (FPP) optic sensor capture. A low-angle shot from the wooden floorboards of a minimalist Tokyo hotel room. The view is directed toward a large floor-to-ceiling glass door that opens onto a balcony. Beyond the glass, the soft textures of spring cherry blossom petals are scattered on the concrete, and a sliver of the Shibuya skyline is visible in the far distance under a pale blue afternoon sky. The lighting is soft and natural. World is impossibly large. No text or overlays.

First-person perspective (FPP) optic sensor capture. A macro view of a weathered wooden table at a quiet cafe in Tomigaya, Tokyo. A single, small QR code sticker is adhered to the textured wood grain, reflecting a hint of neon magenta from a nearby sign. The background is a soft bokeh of a quiet, tree-lined street. The focus is sharp on the intersection of the ancient wood and the digital code. World is impossibly large. No text or overlays.
